close_reason: Decided + documented: PlanetBids HTTP scanner is a no-op vs the modern SPA (moduledoc note added); coverage requires authenticated browser session (creds, gyl.6); papi/OAuth deferred.
description: LATENT BUG: do_planetbids_scan (scan_planetbids.ex hits /portal/{id}/bo/bo-search, Floki-parses) gets WAF-blocked/empty against modern PlanetBids -> 0 bids; only the browser fallback works. PlanetBids is Ember.js: WAF passes with Chrome UA but data loads from a papi API namespace behind OAuth token-exchange (endpoint in a lazy-loaded chunk). Decide: (a) browser (pragmatic, works today), or (b) reverse-engineer papi + OAuth (robust but fragile/high-effort). Either fix or explicitly retire the dead HTTP scanner.

comments.text: DECISION (informed by gyl.3): modern PlanetBids = AWS WAF + Ember SPA + papi OAuth API. Headless browser passes WAF but the Ember app never renders bid data headless (empty body). Plain HTTP gets the SPA shell only. So: (a) the existing do_planetbids_scan / ScanPlanetBids HTTP path is effectively a no-op against current PlanetBids -- documented as such in the moduledoc (commit just landed), kept for legacy/server-rendered portals + as a cheap first attempt; (b) real PlanetBids coverage needs an AUTHENTICATED BROWSER SESSION (the system already has requires_login + planetbids_login_js + SourceCredentials) -- gated on real creds (see gyl.6); (c) papi/OAuth reverse-engineering deferred as high-effort/fragile.

title: PlanetBids coverage: fix dead HTTP path; decide browser vs papi API
